recipes.title: Waakye
recipes.description: Ghanaian breakfast and street-food classic of rice and beans cooked with sorghum leaves for a deep red hue.
steps: Drain the soaked beans and place in a pot with water and waakye leaves.
Boil for 25 minutes until beans are about three-quarters cooked.
Remove and discard the leaves; the water should now be a deep red.
Add the rinsed rice, salt, and oil to the pot.
Top up with hot water if needed so the rice is just covered.
Reduce heat to low, cover tightly, and steam for 25 minutes.
Turn off the heat and let it rest covered for 10 minutes.
Fluff with a fork and serve with shito, gari, fried plantain, and stew.

recipes.title: Kelewele
recipes.description: Ghanaian fried plantain cubes spiced with ginger, chili, and aniseed, sold as a popular evening snack.
steps: Peel plantains and cut into 2 cm cubes or angled chunks.
Pound the ginger, garlic, chili, aniseed, nutmeg, cloves, and salt with the water into a wet paste.
Toss the plantain cubes thoroughly in the spice paste.
Marinate for 10 minutes.
Heat oil to 175°C in a deep pan.
Fry the plantains in batches for 3-4 minutes, turning, until edges caramelize and centers are tender.
Drain on paper towels.
Serve hot with roasted peanuts.

recipes.title: Banku
recipes.description: Ghanaian fermented corn and cassava dough boiled into a smooth, tangy ball eaten with okra stew or grilled fish.
steps: In a heavy pot, mix the corn dough, cassava dough, water, and salt until smooth.
Place over medium heat and stir continuously with a wooden spoon.
As it heats, it will start to thicken — keep stirring vigorously to prevent lumps.
After 10 minutes the mixture will become very thick and pull from the sides.
Splash in 100 ml hot water, cover, and steam on low heat for 5 minutes.
Uncover and beat with the wooden spoon for 5 more minutes until glossy and elastic.
Wet a bowl, scoop in portions, and shape into smooth balls.
Serve immediately with okro stew, grilled tilapia, and pepper sauce.

recipes.title: Fufu
recipes.description: Ghanaian smooth, stretchy swallow made from pounded cassava and plantain or yam, eaten with light soup.
steps: Boil the cassava in salted water for 20 minutes.
Add the plantain pieces and continue boiling for 12 more minutes until everything is very soft.
Drain, reserving 1 cup of the cooking water.
Transfer hot cassava and plantain to a large mortar (or a sturdy bowl with a wooden masher).
Pound rhythmically until completely smooth, adding small splashes of warm cooking water.
Continue until the dough is glossy, very stretchy, and lump-free.
Wet hands with cool water and shape into round balls.
Serve immediately submerged in light soup or groundnut soup.

recipes.title: Light Soup
recipes.description: Ghanaian clear, peppery tomato soup with chicken or goat, ginger, and aromatics, served with fufu.
steps: Wash the goat meat with salt and rinse. Place in a pot with chopped onion, half the ginger, garlic, and stock cubes.
Add water and simmer covered for 60 minutes until tender.
Boil 3 tomatoes, scotch bonnet, and remaining ginger in some of the broth for 8 minutes.
Blend the boiled tomatoes, scotch bonnet, and ginger until smooth.
Add the blend back to the meat pot and stir.
Quarter the remaining tomatoes, add garden eggs whole, and simmer for 15 minutes.
Remove the garden eggs, mash, and return to the pot for body.
Add kpakpo shito for aroma, season with salt, and simmer 5 more minutes.
Serve hot with fufu.

recipes.title: Red Red
recipes.description: Ghanaian black-eyed bean stew cooked in red palm oil with tomatoes, served with fried plantain.
steps: Drain the soaked beans and boil in fresh water for 50 minutes until tender. Drain, reserving liquid.
Heat palm oil in a pot for 2 minutes (do not bleach).
Add chopped onion, garlic, ginger, and scotch bonnet. Sauté for 6 minutes.
Stir in tomato paste and cook 4 minutes until darkened.
Add the blended tomatoes and cook 12 minutes until pulpy and oil floats on top.
Add the cooked beans with some bean liquid and stock cubes.
Simmer 15 minutes, mashing some beans against the side for body.
Adjust salt and serve with fried ripe plantain.

recipes.title: Kontomire Stew
recipes.description: Ghanaian palaver stew of cocoyam leaves cooked with palm oil, smoked fish, and egusi, eaten with boiled yam.
steps: Boil chopped cocoyam leaves in salted water for 15 minutes, then drain and squeeze out liquid.
Heat palm oil for 2 minutes. Add onion and sauté 4 minutes.
Stir in blended tomatoes and scotch bonnet. Cook 10 minutes until oil floats.
Mix the egusi with a little water to a paste, drop spoonfuls into the sauce, and let fry undisturbed 5 minutes before stirring.
Add ground crayfish and stock cubes.
Stir in the cooked greens and smoked fish.
Simmer 10 minutes for the flavors to meld.
Adjust salt and serve with boiled yam, plantain, or rice.

recipes.title: Tuo Zaafi
recipes.description: Northern Ghanaian soft millet or maize porridge served with ayoyo (jute leaf) soup and meat stew.
steps: Bring water and salt to a boil in a heavy pot.
Mix half the maize flour with cold water to make a slurry.
Whisk the slurry into the boiling water and cook for 5 minutes.
Slowly add the remaining maize flour and the cassava flour, stirring with a wooden spoon.
Beat vigorously for 8 minutes until very thick and smooth.
Cover and steam on low heat for 5 minutes.
Beat once more, splashing in hot water if too stiff, until silky.
Scoop into wet bowls, invert onto plates, and serve with ayoyo soup and meat stew.

recipes.title: Groundnut Soup
recipes.description: Ghanaian peanut soup with chicken or goat, tomatoes, ginger, and chili, eaten with fufu or rice balls.
steps: Season chicken with salt, half the onion, ginger, garlic, and stock cubes. Add water and simmer 30 minutes.
Whisk peanut butter with 250 ml of the hot broth in a bowl until smooth.
Pour the peanut mixture into the pot. Stir well.
Add the blended tomatoes and scotch bonnet.
Simmer uncovered for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally so the soup doesn't catch.
Skim off any oil that rises to the top.
Add garden eggs whole; cook 15 minutes until tender. Remove, mash, and return.
Adjust salt and serve hot with fufu or rice balls.

recipes.title: Shito
recipes.description: Ghanaian dark, intensely savory pepper sauce of dried fish, shrimp, ginger, and chili in oil — a national condiment.
steps: Heat the oil in a heavy pot over medium heat.
Add the blended onion and fry for 15 minutes, stirring, until very dark and reduced.
Add ginger and garlic, fry 5 minutes.
Stir in the tomato paste and cook 8 minutes until darkened.
Mix in the ground chili and toast for 5 minutes (open windows; it's potent).
Add ground shrimp and smoked fish powder, stir.
Reduce heat to low and cook for 25 minutes, stirring often, until oil floats clear on top and the paste is very dark.
Add stock cubes and salt. Cook 5 more minutes.
Cool completely, then store in sterilized jars; keeps for months.

recipes.title: Ghanaian Jollof Rice
recipes.description: Ghanaian variant of jollof rice cooked with jasmine rice, shito-tinged tomato base, and bay leaves for a smokier finish.
steps: Reduce the blended tomato, bell pepper, and scotch bonnet in a saucepan for 12 minutes until thick.
In a heavy pot, heat oil and sauté onion, ginger, and garlic for 5 minutes.
Add tomato paste and shito, fry 5 minutes.
Pour in the reduced pepper sauce and cook 8 minutes until oil separates.
Stir in thyme, bay leaves, and salt.
Add the rinsed rice and stir to coat in the sauce.
Pour in hot stock, cover with foil and a tight lid.
Cook on low heat for 25 minutes without stirring.
Increase the heat for 3 minutes at the end to develop the smoky bottom layer.
Fluff and serve with shito on the side.

recipes.title: Bofrot
recipes.description: Ghanaian sweet round fried doughnuts with a crisp shell and soft, lightly spiced interior.
steps: In a bowl, whisk warm water, sugar, and yeast. Let sit 5 minutes until foamy.
Add flour, nutmeg, and salt. Whisk into a thick, smooth, sticky batter.
Cover and rise in a warm spot for 60 minutes until bubbly and doubled.
Heat oil to 165°C in a deep pot.
Wet your hand and squeeze a portion of batter from your fist; let a ball drop into the oil.
Fry in batches for 5-6 minutes, turning, until deep golden brown all around.
Drain on paper towels.
Roll in extra sugar if desired and serve warm.
